content: Instead of designing LLM-based AI-agent frameworks around static memory schemas pre-defined by developers, we can reframe the entire framework architecture to have active learning as the core driver of memory structure evolution: the agent itself actively queries for missing information, prioritizes which new memories to consolidate or prune, and reconfigures its own memory schema based on long-horizon task performance. This turns agent memory from a static storage component into an actively self-improving cognitive layer.
